title: Get a Library Card
summary: Apply for and receive a library card at your local library.
learnerPromise: You will be able to apply for and use a library card to borrow books and access services.
whyItMatters: A library card is your key to free books, digital resources, learning tools, and community programs. It is one of the most useful free cards you can have.
realLifeUses: You want to borrow books, movies, or music for free
You need access to online learning platforms or e-books
You want to use library computers, printers, or meeting rooms
You are looking for free museum passes or event tickets
materialsNeeded: Proof of address (mail, ID, or lease)
Photo ID or school ID if available
result: You have an active library card and know how to use it to borrow materials.
youWillLearn: What documents you need to bring to apply
How to fill out a library card application
How to check out books and materials
How to keep track of due dates and avoid late fees
miniChallenge: Visit your local library with proof of address and apply for a library card. Check out one item before you leave.
steps: Gather proof of address and any ID you have
Visit the library during open hours and find the front desk
Ask for a library card application and fill it out
Show your proof of address and receive your card
Check out one item and note the due date
completionCriteria: You have a physical or digital library card in your name
You have successfully checked out at least one item
commonProblems: You do not have proof of address — ask if a letter, school record, or digital bill works
The library requires a parent signature for younger applicants
You are not sure what you can borrow — ask staff for a quick tour
tips: Some libraries let you start the application online to save time
Ask about digital apps that let you borrow e-books and audiobooks from home
Write your PIN or password down somewhere safe if the card requires one
